title: Ice Cream Of Margie With The Light Blue

jokes.text: I only eat the clown heads. That's so wasteful. I've always done it. Just like I only eat the eyes off a lobster.
jokes.setup: Bart is wastefully eating only part of his food
jokes.punchline: He escalates to an even more bizarre and specific eating habit with lobster eyes
jokes.explanation: Great character moment for Bart's bizarre logic, with good escalation from clown heads to lobster eyes

jokes.text: Your father works very hard to put lobsters on our table. Every night, he comes home exhausted, with his voice too hoarse to talk.
jokes.setup: Marge defending Homer's work ethic
jokes.punchline: The implication that Homer comes home 'too hoarse to talk' suggests he's been drinking/goofing off, not working hard
jokes.explanation: Subtle character comedy showing Marge's naive defense of Homer's questionable work habits

jokes.text: I'm the king of goofing off!
jokes.setup: Homer is at work presumably being productive
jokes.punchline: He openly declares himself king of not working
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer character moment, though fairly predictable

jokes.text: I thought we were playing Cincinnati Time Waste. Oh, in that case...
jokes.setup: Confusion over what workplace 'game' they're playing
jokes.punchline: The made-up game name 'Cincinnati Time Waste' perfectly describes what they're actually doing
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that captures the absurdity of workplace time-wasting games

jokes.text: When you pass the coffee machine, you're supposed to yell 'hot pot' and eat a sugar cube.
jokes.setup: Explaining arbitrary workplace game rules
jokes.punchline: The completely random and nonsensical rule involving shouting and eating sugar
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist humor showing how arbitrary workplace rules can be

jokes.text: On your feet, you sniggering oran-gu-tan.
jokes.setup: Burns addressing Homer angrily
jokes.punchline: His pretentious pronunciation of 'orangutan' with hyphenated syllables
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns character moment with his affected speech patterns

jokes.text: My fat friend, you are hanging by a very thin thread.
jokes.setup: Burns threatening Homer's job
jokes.punchline: The visual pun of a 'thin thread' supporting someone fat
jokes.explanation: Clever visual wordplay that works on multiple levels

jokes.text: Come and lick me, big boy. I said lick me!
jokes.setup: Ice cream truck trying to get Homer's attention
jokes.punchline: The sexually suggestive language from an ice cream truck
jokes.explanation: Subversive humor turning innocent ice cream truck into something suggestive

jokes.text: Ew! You're not ice cream! Faker.
jokes.setup: Homer realizes he was talking to Burns, not ice cream
jokes.punchline: His childish disappointment and accusation of 'faker'
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's childlike logic

jokes.text: Change a hundred? Sure. That's $1.50, $1.75, $2.00...
jokes.setup: Homer casually asks to change a hundred for ice cream
jokes.punchline: Ice cream man slowly counting out change in quarters
jokes.explanation: Good escalating physical comedy setup showing the absurdity of the situation

jokes.text: Max loved to talk about his work. How he bored me.
jokes.setup: Widow talking about her deceased husband
jokes.punchline: Her blunt admission that he bored her
jokes.explanation: Dark humor with perfect deadpan delivery about a dead husband

jokes.text: Why, doesn't he need it? Oh, right.
jokes.setup: Widow says she'll sell the ice cream truck
jokes.punchline: Homer's delayed realization that dead people don't need trucks
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer slow-on-the-uptake moment with death

jokes.text: Otto, you totally pimped Dad's ride! He was following my design
jokes.setup: Otto has modified Homer's ice cream truck
jokes.punchline: Using 'pimped' in the MTV sense, while Lisa takes credit
jokes.explanation: Dated pop culture reference with mild character moment

jokes.text: Today, we're celebrating women who didn't waste their lives being mommies.
jokes.setup: Talk show introduction
jokes.punchline: Bluntly calling motherhood 'wasting your life'
jokes.explanation: Sharp satirical take on feminist empowerment talk shows

jokes.text: It's about women who want to breast-feed their own dreams and change the poopy diapers of fulfillment. I call it leaving your 'lega-she.'
jokes.setup: Professor explaining her book
jokes.punchline: Mixing baby metaphors with 'legacy' becoming 'lega-she'
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay though the baby metaphors are a bit overwrought

jokes.text: Now her husband asks her for money or as I call it: 'mon-she.'
jokes.setup: Continuing the gender-bent wordplay pattern
jokes.punchline: Money becomes 'mon-she'
jokes.explanation: Weaker follow-up to the lega-she joke, feeling forced

jokes.text: I feel like Icarus, flying too close to Apollo's golden orb.
jokes.setup: Homer commenting on the hot weather
jokes.punchline: His surprisingly erudite mythological reference
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Homer's occasional unexpected intelligence

jokes.text: Product contains neither ice nor cream. May contain trace elements of Mexican cheese. Do not consume.
jokes.setup: Homer reading ice cream truck warnings
jokes.punchline: The escalating disclaimers ending with 'do not consume'
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalating absurdity that parodies food safety warnings

jokes.text: I'm a unitard.
jokes.setup: Ralph in a wrestling outfit
jokes.punchline: Confusing 'unitard' with 'you retard'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ralph moment with innocent wordplay confusion

jokes.text: Son, I will not stand for intolerance.
jokes.setup: Kid says he's lactose intolerant
jokes.punchline: Homer treating it as social intolerance
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay showing Homer's misunderstanding with social justice overtones

jokes.text: You give this delightful frozen treat the same respect you would give any lactose-free food. God bless America.
jokes.setup: Homer's anti-intolerance speech
jokes.punchline: Turning ice cream into patriotic duty
jokes.explanation: Strong follow-through that escalates the absurdity to patriotic proportions.

jokes.text: Ooh, thomeone wearth a brathelet. Who'th your boyfriend? Uh... Dr. Osterberg?
jokes.setup: Nelson mocking a kid's medical bracelet
jokes.punchline: Kid's confused response naming his doctor
jokes.explanation: Good character moment for Nelson with innocent kid confusion

jokes.text: I just wish I could do something to be remembered by, something that says 'Marge was here' after I'm gone. Oh, honey, what about the time... Everybody loved that.
jokes.setup: Marge expressing existential longing
jokes.punchline: Homer's vague, unconvincing reassurance
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Homer's inability to remember specific supportive examples

jokes.text: Ah, the story of my life. They get the Popsicles, I get the sticks.
jokes.setup: Marge cleaning up popsicle sticks
jokes.punchline: Her melancholy observation about always getting the leftovers
jokes.explanation: Poignant character moment that perfectly captures Marge's domestic martyrdom

jokes.text: Will America accept a British James Bond? I can't wait to find out.
jokes.setup: Homer suggesting making a James Bond sculpture
jokes.punchline: His obliviousness that Bond has always been British
jokes.explanation: Decent meta humor about Homer's pop culture ignorance

jokes.text: You think people would be Kent-rested in me? I do.
jokes.setup: Kent's punny show name
jokes.punchline: Marge innocently using the pun herself
jokes.explanation: Mild pun humor with good character interaction

jokes.text: My God, it's beauty-ful. I finally see the goodness in myself. Come here, you.
jokes.setup: Moe sees his popsicle stick sculpture
jokes.punchline: His overwhelming emotional response to seeing himself positively
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moe character moment showing his deep insecurities and need for validation

jokes.text: You're not alone anymore. No you ain't. Aw, you're shivering, are you cold?
jokes.setup: Moe bonding with his sculpture
jokes.punchline: His increasingly intimate relationship with the wooden figure
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy showing Moe's loneliness taken to extremes

jokes.text: This is the most fun I've ever had giving you wood. Homie, this sculpting has made me feel so fulfilled. I'm as hopeful and full of life as I was in the years before I met you.
jokes.setup: Homer's innocent comment about giving Marge popsicle sticks
jokes.punchline: Marge's brutal honesty about being happier before marriage
jokes.explanation: Dark relationship humor with double entendre and brutal honesty about marriage

jokes.text: Shoot on the 'yee,' not the 'hah.'
jokes.setup: Rich Texan's celebration
jokes.punchline: His specific instructions about when to fire guns during 'yeehaw'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character-specific humor showing Texan's precision about stereotypical behavior

jokes.text: Hey, I do more than TV. I had a one-man show on Broadway. That's who showed up-- one man.
jokes.setup: Troy bragging about his Broadway show
jokes.punchline: Revealing only one person attended
jokes.explanation: Excellent self-deprecating humor with perfect Troy McClure delivery

jokes.text: Our marriage is like soft-serve ice cream. And trust is the hard chocolate shell that keeps it from melting on to our carpet. In 'cone-clusion,' here's the scoop: I love you.
jokes.setup: Homer making an extended marriage metaphor
jokes.punchline: Multiple ice cream puns culminating in 'cone-clusion'
jokes.explanation: Homer's extended metaphor with multiple puns, slightly overwrought but character-appropriate

jokes.text: Attention, losers: this is your chance to buy your kids some frozen love. I'm not going to fall for such a cheap stunt. Mom would.
jokes.setup: Homer's crude sales pitch
jokes.punchline: Kid's casual observation about his mom's susceptibility
jokes.explanation: Good sales pitch humor with realistic family dynamic

jokes.text: Sorry, I'm all out of ice cream. Already? It's only 38 seconds to 3:00.
jokes.setup: Homer sold out of ice cream
jokes.punchline: The ridiculously specific timing showing how fast he sold out
jokes.explanation: Good timing humor showing Homer's unexpected success

jokes.text: I don't know where Carl ends and I begin. See, statements like that are why people think we're gay.
jokes.setup: Lenny making an intimate statement about Carl
jokes.punchline: Carl's meta-awareness of how their relationship appears
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta-humor addressing fan speculation about Lenny and Carl's relationship

jokes.text: Well, excuse me for having enormous flaws that I don't work on!
jokes.setup: Homer defending himself to Marge
jokes.punchline: His brutally honest self-assessment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer character moment with quotable self-awareness about his own flaws

jokes.text: It was giving you those sticks, and watching you smile as you turned them into art. If it would win you back, I would gradually eat all the parts of a motorcycle.
jokes.setup: Homer's heartfelt apology
jokes.punchline: His absurd offer to eat a motorcycle
jokes.explanation: Great Homer moment mixing genuine emotion with absurd physical comedy

jokes.text: I'm laughing in the last one, but it's only 'cause the cat fell down the stairs.
jokes.setup: Homer sending sad photos to Marge
jokes.punchline: Explaining why one photo shows him happy
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor mixing Homer's sadness with slapstick animal comedy

jokes.text: Did you change your name? Is it Kelly? Kelly!
jokes.setup: Homer desperately searching for Marge
jokes.punchline: His random guess that she changed her name to Kelly
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Homer's desperate, illogical thinking

jokes.text: A complete list of things I have seen or not seen is available on my blog. Your mother is on the 'not seen' list, along with 'a Star Wars film that was any good since the first one.' And even that has been ruined by CGI additions. Bravo, George.
jokes.setup: Kids asking if he's seen their mother
jokes.punchline: His elaborate complaint about Star Wars prequels and special editions
jokes.explanation: Perfect Comic Book Guy moment though the Star Wars complaints feel dated

jokes.text: A complete list of things I have seen or not seen is available on my blog. Your mother is on the 'not seen' list, along with 'a Star Wars film that was any good since the first one.'
jokes.setup: Kids asking Comic Book Guy if he's seen their mom
jokes.punchline: Unnecessarily elaborate response with Star Wars criticism
jokes.explanation: Perfect Comic Book Guy response that turns simple question into opportunity for pedantic pop culture rant.

jokes.text: You made me a sculpture of Magilla Gorilla. No, Homer. It's you-- my sweet, perfectly imperfect you.
jokes.setup: Homer misidentifying his own sculpture
jokes.punchline: Marge's loving correction
jokes.explanation: Sweet character moment with Homer's self-deprecation

jokes.text: Now I know that my lega-she is really a lega-we.
jokes.setup: Returning to the feminist book's terminology
jokes.punchline: Marge adapting it to include her family
jokes.explanation: Good callback that gives the earlier wordplay emotional payoff

jokes.text: Not that one!
jokes.setup: Kids diving into dumpster for ice cream
jokes.punchline: Homer's warning about the wrong dumpster
jokes.explanation: Standard physical comedy ending

jokes.text: This statue, subject unknown, is the last remaining example of art before the collapse of the western world. If only we'd known that iPods would unite and overthrow the very humans they entertained.
jokes.setup: Future dystopian narrator
jokes.punchline: iPods taking over the world
jokes.explanation: Decent sci-fi parody though feels disconnected from main episode

jokes.text: What do you want from us?! Nothing. We just like whipping.
jokes.setup: Human pleading with iPod overlords
jokes.punchline: iPods just enjoy the cruelty
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about technology overlords, though ending feels tacked on
